items.title: Lakers add Matisse Thybulle to fill weak spot, but create roster crunch
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers are making a low-stakes bet on Matisse Thybulle.The 29-year-old wing known for his elite defensive skills is the latest addition to the Lakers' offseason roster overhaul, according to ESPN, after agreeing a one-year deal with the team on Monday, July 20.Thybulle is seen as a potential fit as part of the Lakers' wing rotation due to his versatility as a defender and a 3-point shot that improved since earlier in his career. He has also struggled to remain healthy in recent seasons and his addition leaves the Lakers with at least one player too many under contract heading into the 2026-27 campaign. Yet it's still far from certain Thybulle can be a viable option alongside Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves.Here's what to know about Thybulle's signing with the Lakers, including contract details, the team's current roster and what it means for the franchise moving…

It’s been roughly four weeks since James announced he would not be returning to the Los Angeles Lakers. The King will be moving on after eight years wearing the purple and gold. And now, we are all still waiting for him to announce where his talents will be taken…

items.title: A List Of NBA Draft Picks Out Of Villanova
items.summary: Villanova has enjoyed great success and put itself in the conversation of blue blood territory in recent years.This success dates back decades as the Wildcats have been sending players to the next level since 1950.That year, Paul Arizin was selected third-overall by the Philadelphia Warriors. To this day, he remains as the highest-drafted player to come out of Villanova.Arizin was also the first of nine players selected in the Top 10 in the NBA Draft and one of 66 total players. The 2018 class had the most selections with four. Mikal Bridges led the way that year as the 10th overall selection while Donte DiVincenzo and Omari Spellman joined him in the first round. Jalen Brunson went on to be a second round selection as well.With the addition of Luigi Suigo and others, it looks as though the list will grow in 2027 but for now, this is how it looks:YearRoundPickPlayerTeam195013Paul…

items.title: Lakers agree to 1-year deal with former All-Defensive wing Matisse Thybulle
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to terms on a one-year, $3.3 million contract with veteran wing defender Matisse Thybulle, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.After finishing last season ranked 18th in defensive efficiency, L.A. saw its best perimeter stopper, veteran guard Marcus Smart, decamp for Houston early in free agency. With Luka Dončić beginning the three-year extension he signed last summer and Austin Reaves now in the fold for the next four years, the Lakers have a backcourt tilted heavily toward the offensive end of the floor, and in need of some wings capable of getting stops and creating turnovers up top to prevent new franchise center Walker Kessler from having to turn away a steady stream of drivers possession after possession. And with Kessler receiving a nine-figure deal of his own in the sign-and-trade that moved him from Utah to California, they needed those…

items.title: Lakers sign Matisse Thybulle to a one-year deal as roster overhaul continues
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers guard Matisse Thybulle in the second half of a game in April 2025 in Denver. (David Zalubowski / Associated Press)The Lakers have signed forward Matisse Thybulle to a one-year, $3.3-million contract, people familiar with the deal but not authorized to speak publicly told The Times Monday.Thybulle, who spent last season playing for the Portland Trail Blazers, made the NBA’s all-defensive team twice over his seven-year career. He is a defensive wing player that the Lakers sought to surround Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves.Thybulle averaged 5.8 points per game last season and shot 43.3% from the field, 39.8% from three-point range, in 30 games.The Lakers now have a 16-man roster. They’ll have to either trade or waive a player to be at the league maximum number of 15.Sign up for our weekly newsletter on all things Lakers.This story originally appeared in Los…

items.title: Lakers reportedly sign Matisse Thybulle to one-year minimum contract
items.summary: After rumors of their interest had been swirling for a week, the Lakers have agreed to sign defensive wing Matisse Thybulle to a one-year, $3.3 million veteran minimum contract, reports Shams Charania of ESPN.The Lakers are looking for 3&D wings to play around Luka Doncic and, on paper, Thybulle fits that bill perfectly. He is a high-level perimeter defender, a two-time All-Defensive Team player (most recently 2022), and in the last two seasons he shot 43.8% and then 39.8% from 3-point range. Thybulle long had the reputation of an elite defender who became tougher to use in the playoffs because of his offensive liabilities, but the signs of improved offense the past couple of years could change that narrative.The concern here is health — Thybulle has played a total of 45 games over those last two seasons. He missed time last season following a thumb surgery (torn ulnar collateral…

items.title: NetsDaily Off-Season Report – No. 14
items.summary: Yeah, we’re a little late but there’s a lot to ponder.The end of the Brooklyn Nets two summer leagues, coinciding with the start of free agency, seems like a good time to assess things. The Nets could make a move yet and LeBron James hasn’t made his decision. Just Saturday, the New York Knicks tendered an offer sheet to the Dallas Mavericks for Moussa Cisse, a 6’11” 23-year-old center, only to see it matched hours later. On Sunday, the Mavs, Thunder and Hawks did a three-way swap that sent All-Defensive Wing Lu Dort from OKC to Atlanta, former overall No. 1 pick Zaccharie Risacher from Atlanta to the Dallas, Ryan Nemhard from Dallas to Atlanta and multiple draft picks, what else?, to OKC.Things change. Also, the Nets won’t play now for nearly three months, sometime the first week of October when training camp and preseason open. So where are we?The Nets two lottery picks from 2025 and…

items.title: NBA Offseason Trade/Free Agent Rumors 2026: Lakers, Hawks not close to Kuminga deal, Jalen Duren latest
items.summary: While the NBA continues to wait for LeBron James to announce where he's going to play next season — and there is NBA business held up because of it — there are still other talks and things going on. Here are some of the latest non-LeBron rumors.Lakers, Hawks not close on Jonathan KumingaThere is a segment of Lakers fans talking them into Jonathan Kuminga as a difference-maker, likely the same fans who talked themselves into Deandre Ayton being a difference-maker a year ago despite a long history suggesting otherwise (which echoes Kuminga).That may not be an issue because the Lakers and Hawks are nowhere close to a deal, reports Dan Woike at The Athletic."...it appears that momentum has stalled with the sides far apart on terms and L.A. not overly motivated to push significant draft capital into sign-and-trade scenarios. Still, he appears to be the biggest upside play left on…
